clinical_picture: Active violent behavior; continuous seizure from substance withdrawal; unconsciousness from overdose; acute, severe self-harm requiring life-saving medical care.

presenting_complaints.name: Violent/Homicidal Behaviour
presenting_complaints.description: Severe, unmanageable aggression with explicit intent to kill or severely maim another person

presenting_complaints.name: Bizarre Behaviour
presenting_complaints.description: Completely uncontrolled, hyper-agitated, or chaotic actions that pose an immediate risk of physical devastation,

secondary_modifiers.name: Imminent Safety Risk
secondary_modifiers.description: Active physical violence occurring at triage (e.g., brandishing a weapon, physically assaulting staff or bystanders).

secondary_modifiers.name: Severe Agitation
secondary_modifiers.description: Extreme psychomotor agitation, such as running blindly, screaming, or destroying property, making verbal de-escalation impossible.

secondary_modifiers.name: Gross Cognitive/Perceptual Impairment
secondary_modifiers.description: Complete delirium or command hallucinations ordering immediate harm, completely overriding the patient's rationale.

clinical_picture: Expressing explicit intent to end their life with a lethal plan; acute, terrifying psychosis; severe, unpredictable agitation; high risk of flight.

presenting_complaints.name: Depression / Suicidal / Deliberate Self-Harm
presenting_complaints.description: Patient has made a lethal suicide attempt immediately prior to arrival or presents with an active, formulated suicide plan.

presenting_complaints.name: Anxiety / Situational Crisis
presenting_complaints.description: Severe panic, frantic behavior, or state of total emotional collapse.

presenting_complaints.name: Hallucinations / Delusions
presenting_complaints.description: Acute psychosis or highly distressing, terrifying unshared realities.

secondary_modifiers.name: Active Suicidal Intent
secondary_modifiers.description: Expressing a definitive desire to die with access to lethal means or a specific planned method.

secondary_modifiers.name: Uncertain Flight or Safety Risk
secondary_modifiers.description: Expressing an intent to elope from care, combined with an unpredictable mental status that compromises safety

secondary_modifiers.name: Severe Anxiety/Agitation
secondary_modifiers.description: Pacing, extreme restlessness, hyperventilating, or demanding to leave because of intense inner panic

secondary_modifiers.name: Acute Psychosis
secondary_modifiers.description: New-onset hallucinations or delusions that are causing severe distress, paranoia, or highly erratic behavior

clinical_picture: Suicidal ideation without immediate intent or plan; severe depression or anxiety; able to follow directions but in profound distress.

presenting_complaints.name: Depression / Suicidal / Deliberate Self-Harm
presenting_complaints.description: Suicidal thoughts are present, but there is no immediate plan, active intent, or recent attempt

presenting_complaints.name: Anxiety / Situational Crisis
presenting_complaints.description: Moderate anxiety or a profound social crisis, such as active physical/mental abuse or severe situational trauma.

presenting_complaints.name: Hallucinations / Delusions
presenting_complaints.description: Auditory or visual hallucinations that are accompanied by distressing paranoia, but behavior remains cooperative and controlled.

secondary_modifiers.name: Suicidal Ideation
secondary_modifiers.description: No Plan: Passive suicidal ideation (e.g., "I wish I wouldn't wake up") without active plans or immediate intent.

secondary_modifiers.name: Moderate Anxiety/Agitation
secondary_modifiers.description: Visible distress, weeping, or trembling, but the patient remains cooperative, communicative, and responsive to verbal reassurance.

secondary_modifiers.name: Violent Ideation, No Plan
secondary_modifiers.description: Expressing thoughts of wanting to hurt someone else, but denying any intent, plan, or access to means to carry it out.

secondary_modifiers.name: High Emotional Stress/Abuse
secondary_modifiers.description: Presentation following acute domestic violence or severe psychological trauma requiring urgent psychosocial intervention.

clinical_picture: Mild to moderate anxiety or situational distress; request for medication refills; chronic psychiatric symptoms with no acute changes or safety risks.

presenting_complaints.name: Depression
presenting_complaints.description: Depressive symptoms (e.g., sadness, lethargy, low mood) with an absolute and explicit absence of suicidal ideation.

presenting_complaints.name: Anxiety / Situational Crisis
presenting_complaints.description: Mild, generalized anxiety or a situational problem where the patient feels temporarily unable to cope.

presenting_complaints.name: Insomnia
presenting_complaints.description: Acute sleep deprivation causing distress but no cognitive decompensation.

secondary_modifiers.name: Mild Anxiety/Agitation
secondary_modifiers.description: Nervousness or worry over a specific life stressor; the patient can sit quietly and comfortably wait.

secondary_modifiers.name: Stable Bizarre Behaviour
secondary_modifiers.description: Unusual or eccentric behaviors that are well-contained, harmless, and do not disrupt the environment.

secondary_modifiers.name: Inability to Cope
secondary_modifiers.description: Situational exhaustion or burnout without any indicators of psychosis, self-harm, or aggression.

clinical_picture: Minor, chronic issues; long-standing psychiatric conditions seeking routine evaluation or social support referrals.

presenting_complaints.name: Chronic Mental Health Conditions
presenting_complaints.description: : Long-standing psychiatric conditions seeking routine medical follow-up, prescription renewals, or minor adjustments.

presenting_complaints.name: Insomnia
presenting_complaints.description: Chronic sleep difficulties with no acute changes in mental functioning.

secondary_modifiers.name: Chronic Hallucinations
secondary_modifiers.description: Experiencing regular, baseline voices or visions that are familiar to the patient, non-distressing, and unchanged in pattern.

secondary_modifiers.name: Harmless/Stable Behaviour
secondary_modifiers.description: Baseline eccentricities associated with a known psychiatric history, presenting with no safety risks.

secondary_modifiers.name: Chronic Social/Non-Urgent Problem
secondary_modifiers.description: Requests for long-term housing placement, social work assistance, or routine outpatient referrals without acute medical or psychiatric distress.
